frameworks.name: drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: Fits performance as a drift rate parameter varying across task conditions.

frameworks.name: frontostriatal circuit motivation
frameworks.one_line: Frontostriatal connectivity supports valence-oriented effort-cost computation.

frameworks.name: effort cost computation
frameworks.one_line: Framework for understanding motivational deficits in schizophrenia.

core_question: Does frontostriatal connectivity during reward anticipation predict motivated performance differently in schizophrenia versus healthy controls?
key_assumptions: Drift rate from DDM indexes motivated performance.
Cue-phase connectivity reflects anticipatory motivational processing.
key_findings: Schizophrenia group showed deficit in drift rate.
No group difference in overall connectivity or valence-specific connectivity.
Group x valence x difficulty interaction on right dlPFC-striatal connectivity vs drift rate relationship.
HCs showed more positive connectivity-DR coupling in difficult-rewarded condition than SZ.
interpretations: Frontostriatal connectivity is less predictive of performance in SZ when difficulty and reward are combined.
